title: Olearia subrepanda
taxonomy: Olearia subrepanda is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Olearia, which consists of shrubs and small trees native to Australia and New Zealand. The genus Olearia is part of the tribe Astereae and is closely related to the genera Gnaphalium and Helichrysum. Olearia subrepanda is native to Australia and is often found in sandy soils and coastal areas. It is a shrubby or tree-like plant with narrow, pointed leaves and small white or purple flowers.
